input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [https://dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)
- Install the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest). MAUI ships as a workload on .NET 8+.
- Discover versions: `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 2. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (15.x+ for .NET 8, 16.x+ for .NET 9).
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select --switch /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  ```
- Accept the license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → download iOS simulator runtimes.

## 3. Install Android Dependencies

Two options (pick one):

**Option A — Visual Studio Code + standalone tools (lighter):**
- Install the **Android Command-Line Tools** from [developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only)
- Then accept licenses and install required packages:
  ```
  sdkmanager --licenses
  sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" \
             "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  ```

**Option B — Android Studio (full IDE, recommended):**
- **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- The setup wizard auto-installs the SDK, emulator, and platform tools.
- Note the SDK path (typically `~/Library/Android/sdk`).

Set the environment variable in your shell profile (`~/.zshrc`):
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export PATH="$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator"
```

## 4. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

**Discover available workloads/versions:**
```bash
dotnet workload search maui
dotnet workload list          # shows what's installed
```

## 5. Install an Editor/IDE

Pick one:
- **Visual Studio Code** + the **.NET MAUI Extension** (ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) — lightweight
- **JetBrains Rider** — full IDE with MAUI support built-in

## 6. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Check .NET & workload
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list               # should show "maui"

# Check Xcode
xcodebuild -version                # e.g. "Xcode 16.x"
xcrun simctl list devices available # lists iOS simulators

# Check Android
$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools/adb --version
$ANDROID_HOME/emulator/emulator -list-avds

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios          # iOS build (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android      # Android build

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
emulator -avd <AVD_NAME> &
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## Quick Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| `workload install` fails | Run with `sudo`, or use `dotnet workload update` first |
| iOS build fails with provisioning error | Use automatic provisioning or create a dev cert in Xcode → Settings → Accounts |
| Android SDK not found | Verify `ANDROID_HOME` is set and restart your terminal |
| Emulator won't launch on Apple Silicon | Use `arm64-v8a` system images, not `x86_64` |

**Official docs:** [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)
